---
title: "Agentic AI for Academics"
subtitle: "A Shortened Introduction & Live Demo"
author: "Jon Minton"
date: 2026-03-19
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    hash-type: number
    center: true
    transition: fade
bibliography: ../refs/references.bib
---

## Taste the Cake, Change the Recipe

- The **recipe** = source files (.qmd, .R, .py, config)
- The **cake** = rendered output (HTML slides, PDF, plots)
- Version control tracks the **recipe**. The cake is regenerated on demand.

. . .

The academic anti-pattern: editing the cake directly — cutting bits of Word doc, pasting into another Word doc, emailing "final_v3_FINAL.docx"

. . .

That's **frankencake**. Fragile, unreproducible, and untraceable.

::: {.notes}
The hand-drawn diagrams just covered the conceptual shift from chatbot AI to agentic AI. This slide bridges to the practical: agentic AI works best when you work at the recipe level, not the cake level. Most academics instinctively edit outputs. Everything that follows depends on flipping that habit.
:::

## This Repo Right Now

```
auto-tidy-tuesday/
├── slides-shortened/
│   └── index.qmd        ← this presentation (recipe)
├── slides/
│   └── index.qmd        ← longer version
├── explorations/         ← Tidy Tuesday analyses go here
├── refs/
│   └── references.bib   ← bibliography
├── _quarto.yml           ← project config
├── CLAUDE.md             ← agent conventions (the SOP)
└── .gitignore            ← "don't track the cake"
```

`quarto render` bakes the cake. Git tracks the recipe.

::: {.notes}
Orient the audience before the live demo. Point out CLAUDE.md — it's how the agent knows the project's conventions. This is the workspace the agent will operate in.
:::

## What Shall We Explore?

| Project | Character |
|---|---|
| **Tidy Tuesday** | Pick a dataset, explore from scratch |
| **Ayr Town Centre** | Applied GIS, choropleths, spatial data |
| **Economic Inactivity** | Statistical modelling, demographic variation |

. . .

**Your choice** — all three demonstrate agentic AI in action.

::: {.notes}
Default suggestion is Tidy Tuesday — it's native to this repo and shows the full iterative process from nothing. The sister projects are good for showing how an agent navigates an existing, complex codebase.
:::

## What to Watch For

- How the agent **reads and navigates** the codebase
- How it **asks clarifying questions** and iterates
- How **human judgement** steers the analysis
- Where the AI's reasoning **complements** ours
- Where it **falls short** and needs guidance

. . .

The agent is a collaborator, not an oracle.

::: {.notes}
Give the audience a lens so they know what to pay attention to during the demo. The key insight is that it's a dialogue, not a monologue.
:::

---

## {.center}

::: {style="font-size: 1.5em;"}
**Let's go.**
:::

---

# What We Did {.section visibility="hidden"}

::: {.notes}
Unhide this section at the end if time allows and if using Tidy Tuesday. The following slides can be populated during or after the demo.
:::

## What We Explored

*[To be filled in after the live demo]*

- **Dataset**:
- **Output format**:
- **Key findings**:

## What the Agent Did Well

*[To be filled in after the live demo]*

-
-
-

## Where It Needed Us

*[To be filled in after the live demo]*

-
-
-

## Links & Resources

| | |
|---|---|
| **This repo** | [github.com/JonMinton/auto-tidy-tuesday](https://github.com/JonMinton/auto-tidy-tuesday) |
| **Hand-drawn diagrams** | [jonminton.github.io/jon-blog/...](https://jonminton.github.io/jon-blog/posts/handdrawn-stats/ai-and-agentic-ai/) |
| **Tidy Tuesday** | [tidytues.day](https://tidytues.day) |
| **Claude Code** | [claude.ai/claude-code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) |
| **Quarto** | [quarto.org](https://quarto.org) |
